settlementUsed.note: 2026-06-11 = next Argentine business day after 2026-06-10 (Thursday, no holiday)

method.irr: effective annual, actual/365 exponent, bisection on sum(cf/(1+r)^(days/365)) = dirty price
method.cashflows: per 100 VN from specs_bopreal.json remainingCashflows (USD; 30/360 coupons, Serie 1 2x50% amortization 2027, Serie 4 bullet 2028); discounted to expectedPayDate (business-day rolled); scheduled-date variant computed as sensitivity (<0.01pp difference)
method.priceBasis: data912 D-ticker = dirty USD MEP price per 100 VN (BYMA standard); BOPREAL D-tickers compress the 5-char base (BPOA7 -> BPA7D); verified dirty via IAMC control (paridad% x valor tecnico USD reproduces published TIR within rounding) and via implied MEP coherence (1442-1460 across all six lines)
method.valuation: plain USD contractual cashflows; embedded holder puts (1A/1B/1C/4A) and poder cancelatorio NOT modeled, matching IAMC/bonistas TIR convention

sources: https://www.iamc.com.ar/Informe/InformeDiarioTitulosPublicos09062026/ (golden published TIR/MD, via market_context.json; local copy research/iamc_titulos_publicos_2026-06-09.pdf)

results.note: published TIR source: IAMC informe 2026-06-09 (close, settle 06-10); computed 3.7695% from live D-ticker BPA7D USD dirty price 101.95 (data912 2026-06-10 close, settle 2026-06-11) vs IAMC published 3.64%; part of delta is the one-day price move (IAMC dirty 102.109 vs live 101.95); control on IAMC's own price/settlement reproduces published TIR within -0.02pp (residual = IAMC 1-decimal rounding of paridad/VT); bonistas 24hs cross-check TIR 3.6136% (delta +0.16pp); computed MD 1.075y vs IAMC 1.1y. Plain-USD-cashflow valuation per instruction (holder put / poder cancelatorio not modeled).

published TIR source: IAMC informe 2026-06-09 (close, settle 06-10); computed 3.6784% from live D-ticker BPB7D USD dirty price 102.05 (data912 2026-06-10 close, settle 2026-06-11) vs IAMC published 3.79%; part of delta is the one-day price move (IAMC dirty 102.008 vs live 102.05); control on IAMC's own price/settlement reproduces published TIR within -0.08pp (residual = IAMC 1-decimal rounding of paridad/VT); bonistas 24hs cross-check TIR 3.6199% (delta +0.06pp); computed MD 1.076y vs IAMC 1.1y. Plain-USD-cashflow valuation per instruction (holder put / poder cancelatorio not modeled).

Plain-USD-cashflow valuation per instruction (holder put / poder cancelatorio not modeled).

Plain-USD-cashflow valuation per instruction (holder put / poder cancelatorio not modeled).

Plain-USD-cashflow valuation per instruction (holder put / poder cancelatorio not modeled).

notes: All 6 golden BOPREAL instruments PASS within +/-0.75pp of the IAMC published TIR; max |delta| = 0.22pp (BPOA8).

Deltas vs golden are dominated by the one-day gap: golden = IAMC 2026-06-09 close / settle 06-10; computed = data912 2026-06-10 close / settle 06-11. Control runs on IAMC's own dirty price (paridad x VT) and settlement reproduce every published TIR within +/-0.09pp, i.e. within IAMC's 1-decimal rounding of paridad and valor tecnico.
Computed modified durations (1.07-1.08y Serie 1; 2.16-2.17y Serie 4) match IAMC published MD (1.1 / 2.2) and bonistas (1.095-1.097 / 2.233).

Price basis confirmed dirty per 100 VN: live D price ~101.95-102.15 vs valor tecnico 100.6 (Serie 1, accrued ~0.56 since 2026-04-30) and 92.25/91.21 vs VT 100.3 (Serie 4); implied MEP from ARS/D pairs is coherent at 1442-1460.
No spec errors found; specs_bopreal.json unchanged. Discounting to expectedPayDate vs scheduledDate moves TIR by <0.01pp.

TIRs computed on plain contractual USD cashflows. The low Serie 1 yields (~3.6%) vs sovereigns (~7.5%) reflect put/tax-cancelation value embedded in market prices, not modeled here (consistent with IAMC/bonistas convention).
